title: Current Banner Reviews
description: You may also wish to check the Ideal Support Card Banner Pulls and Umas to Watch For sections for an idea of what you might need to be saving for in the future, keeping in mind that each scenario will be 4 months long and you can expect to get roughly one pity per month or two. The yearly F2P income was calculated on the JP server to be around 300k Carats. Some things are powerful, but for short periods of time, and so aren’t worth pulling if you aren’t a whale.
html:
You may also wish to check the Ideal Support Card Banner Pulls and Umas to Watch For sections for an idea of what you might need to be saving for in the future, keeping in mind that each scenario will be 4 months long and you can expect to get roughly one pity per month or two. The yearly F2P income was calculated on the JP server to be around 300k Carats. Some things are powerful, but for short periods of time, and so aren’t worth pulling if you aren’t a whale.

Support Banners

There are two kinds of desirable cards. There are cards that give good skills, where you only need one or two copies, like Bijin, and cards that you use to raise your stats, where you want them MLB, like Kitasan. Some cards can fulfill either role, like Creek. If you need the card MLB, you should have two pities before pulling on that banner. If it’s a skill card, pull with one pity ready, but stop once you get the card. Also, banners with two SSRs are generally better to pull on, as getting random off-rate support cards is usually not helpful due to SRs outperforming most low-LB SSRs.

(June 25) Throne + Pasa SR - ahahahaha
One of the biggest bait banners in the game’s history. Throne is very overpowered in MANT and will elevate your runs immensely. Do not pull it. It instantly falls off when the next scenario comes out, where you can’t easily fit both it and Light Hello in your decks. Borrow it from a whale, and be ready to retrain all your CM umas the day it releases, as it does so in the middle of a CM. Pasa is one of the strongest SR Speed cards, so keep that in mind for your orange limit break gems if you end up pulling one in the future.

People had hoped that they would move this banner to the start of the scenario so it could be used throughout, but alas. Enjoy it for the three weeks it lasts or whatever it is. From here on, each scenario comes with a bespoke support card that’s essentially mandatory for that scenario. Light Hello in the next, Goddesses after that, and so on. They take the slot Throne would take, though there is a small gap between the Grandmasters scenario release and the Goddesses card release where you can use Throne again, one PvP event. In all future scenarios the mandatory card comes at the start of the scenario.

(July 2) Summer Maru + Festa - Situational
Maru is an extremely powerful Speed Card, and you do want her. But, there’s a rerun banner coming up with Maru and Fine Motion (GameTora estimates it coming at the end of next month, very soon), and the upcoming month is very gem heavy.

  • Up to 30k gems for Light Hello
  • Up to 30k gems for Copano Rickey
  • If you like parenting, up to 30k gems for Summer Gold Ship

So, unless you have 120~150k gems or are whaling, you’ll want to wait until that rerun banner to get your Maru. Festa is a really funny card, but not worth pulling for.

(July 7) Bijin + Ikuno Rerun - Skip
Ikuno is the best Guts card in TB, but TB is ending in two weeks, and you won’t be using Guts in Grand Live. And if you want a Wit card, go for the Fine Motion rerun or the upcoming Teio banner.

(July 16) Cafe + Seiun SR - Skip
Single SSR banner. The gold Cafe gives is slightly interesting, but getting enough stamina for Long isn’t a problem in Grand Live.

(July 22) Light Hello + Tachyon - Pull
The first taste of scenario banners. From now on, every scenario will have a banner that gives a Pal/Group card that is mandatory for the scenario. Light Hello is that card here. You can get away with using her R version, but the 0LB SSR is much better. Pull here until you get at least one copy of Light Hello SSR. Tachyon is also a strong Speed card in this scenario, but worse outside of it, so getting her MLB isn’t high priority. Kita + Summer Maru will be fine (or borrow).

Some people don’t like the idea of pulling for these mandatory scenario cards, but getting them means your borrow slot is freed up and you can borrow any other card in the game. So it’s worth it.

(July 27) Turbo + Teio - Situational
Teio is a pretty good Wit card. She gives the same gold skill as Kitasan, so her value does go down if you are using Kita. And there’s the Maru + Fine Motion banner soon that might be more appealing. But you could pull Teio here if you need a Wit card. Note that the next scenario will start with free pulls on a Wit card, and that Wit card is the best. Fine Motion + Ramonu become the Wit cards to use. If you’re planning on pulling Fine Motion’s rerun, your Teio would have a 4 month lifespan.

Uma Banners

Pull if you like the uma, rather than if they’re good. Very few umas are worth pulling solely for strength, the meta shifting often, so having the ones you like is more valuable. If you have to pity a meta uma, it’s not going to pay back those 30,000 Carats you spent. Most will only be useful on two CMs a year or so, there are very few generalist meta umas. A meta longevity of 2 years doesn’t mean the uma will be the best in every CM for 2 years, just that it takes that long for someone to usurp her in her specialty.

There are lots of ways to get umas for free later on, like Daily Legend Races and various pick tickets, so don’t feel too much pressure to pull umas early on. Of course, you can get them as off-rates from later pulls, too. This means pulling on uma banners is better the fewer umas you own, since you have a higher chance of getting new ones off-rate.

For details on the umas, check the reviews here. What will follow is a quick description of the uma so you can get an idea of what the month has in store.

(July 2) Summer McQueen - One of the rare Long-viable Pace Chasers. Her unique scales based on how much stamina you have, like Mejiro Bright. Narita Brian’s alt outclasses her most of the time, but she’s still perfectly viable. Once that alt releases, you’ll want to inherit the white version of Monster on your Summer McQueen. Here’s the tier list for the latest CM on the JP server, which is on Stayer’s Stakes (3600m):

(Blue = Front Runner, Green = Pace Chaser, Yellow = Late Surger, Orange = End Closer)

(July 2) Summer Gold Ship - This is a game changer. The BEST parent for Team Trials for the rest of time due to her unique triggering a gold skill, still highly sought after as a parent on JP. But it’s as a parent. So you can just borrow her. If you love making parents, this might be a must-pull for you. Overview video: Summer Gold Ship - Umamusume Pretty Derby

Outside of Team Trials, she has limited use, mainly being used on debuffers. The unique can trigger your important acceleration skills early, ruining your ace umas. Festival Gold City is the interesting case, where you can use her as an acceleration debuffer in Sprint tracks with this unique.

(July 7) Bamboo Memory - A gambling Late Surger that’s good on tracks where the final leg starts on the final corner, but those tracks tend to be Pace Chaser dominated.

(July 16) Copano Rickey - Best Dirt uma by a lot, and a unique build. She gets stronger the more Green skills you activate. Normal Front Runners try to get 2-3 for groundwork, Rickey wants 6. This is one of the rare umas worth pulling from a meta perspective. Still within the meta on JP.

(July 22) Banana Falcon - Very similar to the OG Smart Falcon. More of a sidegrade, and both are outclassed by Rickey.

(July 27) Steampunk Taishin - A decent End Closer for Medium distance when Straightaway Spurt doesn’t work, particularly after the 2nd anni buffs. Will Global get those early? Who knows. If you want to use End Closer in every CM, she could be the best choice sometimes. But other strategies are usually better on Medium right now.

(July 27) Steampunk Ticket - Better than the OG Winning Ticket, so if you’re a Ticket fan, here you go. But she’s not particularly strong. She’s in the “pretty good” bucket for Medium Late Surgers with 20 other umas.
